entries.content: When extracting a command that already has a shared skill, deepen the skill additively and keep command-owned state mutation (for example adv_project_metadata) in the thin command. Verify skill has no ADV mutator calls while command preserves orchestration and fallback tokens.

Dedicated command extraction can hit both targets when command keeps only phase orchestration and exact approval/mutator tokens, while skill holds bucket heuristics/report templates. adv-cleanup reduced from 244L to 120L with skill at 172L and sync check clean.

Reflection-style commands extract cleanly by leaving archived-change guard, tool calls, and error handling in command while moving rubrics/templates into skill. Preserve non-blocking archive wording in command because it is workflow-critical.

For research-pack commands, keep file-write boundary and exact external tool names in command; move scan rubrics, applicability scoring, and artifact schema to skill. Verification should grep both docs/*-prep.md boundary and Context7/Kagi tool tokens.

For clarification commands already near line target, use missing-skill RED check instead of line-count RED. Keep question-tool and gate/agreement boundary tokens in command; move Socratic taxonomy and smells into skill.

For audit/scanner commands, command should keep quality-gate thresholds and metadata write because they are execution contract; skill can hold dimension rubrics, finding schema, and report schema. Missing-skill RED works for commands already under line target.

Small commands can still benefit from skill extraction for reusable methodology even when command line count already passes KD8. Use missing-skill RED, preserve mutating tool names in command, and keep command at same or lower line count.

For bulk instruction compression, create contract-token snapshot before edits and compare exact token sequences after. Shell single quotes can break Python f-strings using x['tokens']; use item["tokens"] or avoid nested single quotes in bash -c.
